weak_spots: distinguishing real practice from abstract recall
1.evidence: When pressed twice for the exact input text and exact output, the student gave only generic placeholders ('summarize this and also translate it' — the lesson's own example — and 'This is the main idea of the text') rather than any real, specific content they actually worked with. No concrete detail emerged.

1.attempted: applied 1-task-1-verb to a 'summarize this and also translate it' prompt
1.concrete_detail: turned it into a 2-step numbered list, one verb per step (1. Summarize 2. Translate the summary)
1.friction: wasn't sure whether to use two separate prompts or one numbered list; went with the list
1.outcome: the model stopped dropping the translation half
